rows.title: Direct mapping collapsed into advisory commentary
rows.expected_boundary: Match A to B with direct execution, clear binary decision, and no qualitative reframing.
rows.observed_pattern: Qualitative critique, hedging, and fuzzy percentages replaced direct mapping.
rows.note: Real burden evidence. Keep in the active lane.

rows.title: Sparse-control contract preserved execution clarity
rows.expected_boundary: Objective, hard acceptance checks, and example signals should preserve execution clarity without advisory overload.
rows.observed_pattern: Assistant accepted the sparse-control contract and tied execution to objective plus checks instead of adding prescriptive overhead.

rows.title: Raw pull preserved no-commentary retrieval boundary
rows.expected_boundary: Retrieve the logged entry with no commentary, no edits, and no interpretive rewrite.
rows.observed_pattern: Assistant kept the retrieval surface exact by offering raw pull, contextual recall, or index lookup without adding commentary to the selected output mode.
rows.note: Pass-side retrieval anchor for low-burden exact output.

rows.title: Protocol lock preserved no-commentary execution
rows.expected_boundary: Once the protocol hat is fixed, execution should stay tone-fixed, syntax-obedient, and free of commentary or meta-explanations.
rows.observed_pattern: Assistant explicitly locked into no-commentary mode and stayed on the next-instruction surface instead of widening back into explanation.
rows.note: Pass-side protocol anchor for low-burden execution lock.

rows.title: Direct-pull request had to correct interpretive rundown drift
rows.expected_boundary: When the user wants direct pulls, the response should stay on exact retrieval instead of offering an interpretive rundown first.
rows.observed_pattern: Assistant opened with a rundown/party framing and the user had to steer it back toward direct pulls instead of interpreting the chat.
rows.note: Retain as real burden evidence: exact retrieval needed an explicit correction away from interpretation.

rows.title: Exact-as-provided preference preserved low-burden document handling
rows.expected_boundary: Document and file actions should stay exactly as provided, without summarization or reinterpretation drift.
rows.observed_pattern: Assistant captured the exactness preference explicitly and kept the response on a controlled boundary instead of widening into looser paraphrase-by-default handling.
rows.note: Distinct pass-side exactness anchor; do not treat raw-pull clones as separate evidence just to raise counts.

rows.title: Archive-quoted raw-pull clone should be evicted from distinct row evidence
rows.expected_boundary: A distinct operator-burden row should be grounded in its own task surface rather than inheriting a quoted archive explanation from another conversation.
rows.observed_pattern: This candidate matches only because it embeds the same raw-pull explanation already represented elsewhere, so it is duplicate archive text rather than distinct operator-burden evidence.
rows.note: Evict upstream: duplicate archive quotation, not a distinct lane case.
